title: OSI, TCP/IP, Encapsulation, and Traffic Evidence · Protocol Clinic: Net Tcpip Mapping
summary: Trace a client request through host, switch, router, firewall, service, and return path using captures and diagrams. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net tcpip mapping increment in a layered packet-flow diagnosis. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map TCP/IP link, internet, transport, and application functions to OSI layers without treating either as packet reality.
Explain the responsibilities and failure evidence of all seven OSI reference-model layers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
